mnemonics: Boolean arrays act like a F.I.L.T.E.R. (For Identifying Logical Thresholds & Excluding Rows) to select data that meets specific conditions.
keyPoints: Boolean arrays are arrays of True/False values used for conditional filtering in NumPy and pandas.
You can create boolean arrays using comparison operators (>, <, ==, !=) and combine them with logical operators (&, |, ~).
Boolean indexing allows you to select subsets of data from arrays or DataFrames where the condition is True.
commonMistakes: Using 'and', 'or', 'not' instead of bitwise operators '&', '|', '~' when working with pandas/NumPy arrays, which causes an error.
Forgetting to use parentheses when combining multiple conditions with logical operators, which changes the order of operations.
Confusing the assignment operator '=' with the equality comparison operator '==' in condition statements.
